title: gibasis linearis subsp. rhodantha
intro: Gibasis linearis subsp. rhodantha, also known as the Red-flowering Maudlin or Mexican Candelilla, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Acanthaceae family. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Gibasis linearis subsp. rhodantha include its potential to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy.
